theme gallery — dark-2026

bat — Dockerfile

# Palette sample: Dockerfile — instructions, strings, comments, args.
FROM alpine:3.20 AS build

ARG VERSION=1.2.0
ENV APP_HOME=/opt/app

WORKDIR ${APP_HOME}
COPY . .
RUN apk add --no-cache curl \
    && echo "building ${VERSION}" \
    && ./build.sh

EXPOSE 8080
CMD ["./app", "--serve"]

bat — sample.cs

// Palette sample: C# — comments, strings, numbers, keywords, types, xml-doc.
using System;

namespace Palette;

/// <summary>Builds greetings for the palette demo.</summary>
public sealed class Greeter(string name = "world")
{
    private const int MaxRetries = 3;

    public string Greet()
    {
        var sb = new System.Text.StringBuilder();
        for (var i = 0; i < MaxRetries; i++)
        {
            sb.AppendLine($"hello {name} ({i})");
        }
        return sb.ToString();
    }
}

bat — sample.css

/* Palette sample: CSS — at-rules, selectors, properties, values, comments. */
@import "reset.css";

:root {
  --accent: #569cd6;
  --gap: 8px;
}

@media (min-width: 640px) {
  .card#main {
    color: var(--accent);
    margin: 0 auto;
    padding: 1.5rem;
  }
}

bat — sample.go

// Palette sample: Go — comments, strings, numbers, keywords, types, funcs.
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"strings"
)

const maxRetries = 3

// Greeter builds greetings.
type Greeter struct {
	Name string
}

func (g Greeter) Greet() string {
	if g.Name == "" {
		g.Name = "world"
	}
	return fmt.Sprintf("hello %s\n", strings.TrimSpace(g.Name))
}

func main() {
	for i := 0; i < maxRetries; i++ {
		fmt.Print(Greeter{Name: "palette"}.Greet())
	}
}

bat — sample.json

{
  "title": "palette demo",
  "retries": 3,
  "ratio": 0.75,
  "enabled": true,
  "tags": ["alpha", "beta"],
  "server": {
    "host": "127.0.0.1",
    "port": 8080,
    "path": "c:\\logs\\out.txt"
  },
  "nullable": null
}

bat — sample.lua

-- Palette sample: lua — comments, strings, numbers, keywords, functions.
local M = {}

local MAX = 42
local name = "world\n"

--- Doc-style comment describing the greeter.
function M.greet(who)
  who = who or name
  for i = 1, MAX do
    if i % 7 == 0 then
      print(("hello %s (%d)"):format(who, i))
    end
  end
  return true
end

return M

bat — sample.md

# Palette sample: Markdown

A short paragraph with **bold**, _italic_, and `inline code`.

> A blockquote for the Quote scope.

- list item one
- list item two

```python
def greet(name: str) -> str:
    return f"hello {name}"  # fenced code block
```

[a link](https://example.com) and a footnote reference.[^1]

[^1]: the footnote body.

bat — sample.py

"""Palette sample: Python — docstrings, strings, numbers, keywords, types."""
from __future__ import annotations

MAX_RETRIES: int = 3


class Greeter:
    """Builds greetings."""

    def __init__(self, name: str = "world") -> None:
        self.name = name

    def greet(self) -> str:
        parts = [f"hello {self.name} ({i})\n" for i in range(MAX_RETRIES)]
        return "".join(parts)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    print(Greeter("palette").greet())

bat — sample.sh

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Palette sample: shell — comments, strings, vars, keywords, numbers.
set -euo pipefail

readonly MAX_RETRIES=3
greeting="hello\tworld" # escape inside double quotes

retry() {
  local -i attempt=0
  while ((attempt < MAX_RETRIES)); do
    if "$@"; then
      return 0
    fi
    ((attempt += 1))
    printf 'retry %d of %d\n' "$attempt" "$MAX_RETRIES" >&2
  done
  return 1
}

retry curl -fsSL "https://example.com/${greeting}" || echo "gave up"

bat — sample.sql

-- Palette sample: SQL — comments, keywords, strings, numbers, functions.
CREATE TABLE greeting (
    id      INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    name    TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'world',
    ratio   REAL    CHECK (ratio BETWEEN 0.0 AND 1.0)
);

INSERT INTO greeting (id, name) VALUES (1, 'palette');

SELECT id, upper(name) AS shout
FROM greeting
WHERE ratio > 0.5
ORDER BY id DESC
LIMIT 3;

bat — sample.toml

# Palette sample: toml — comments, strings, numbers, booleans, tables.
title   = "palette demo"
retries = 3
ratio   = 0.75
enabled = true

[server]
  host = "127.0.0.1"
  port = 8080
  tags = [ "alpha", "beta" ]

  [server.tls]
    cert = "/etc/ssl/cert.pem"

bat — sample.tsx

// Palette sample: TSX — types, strings, numbers, keywords, JSX, attributes.
import { useState } from "react";

interface Props {
  name?: string;
  retries: number;
}

export function Greeter({ name = "world", retries }: Props): JSX.Element {
  const [count, setCount] = useState<number>(0);
  const label = `hello ${name} (${count}/${retries})`;

  return (
    <button
      className="card"
      disabled={count >= retries}
      onClick={() => setCount((c) => c + 1)}
    >
      {label}
    </button>
  );
}

bat — sample.xml

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE config SYSTEM "config.dtd">
<!-- Palette sample: XML — declaration, DOCTYPE, tags, attributes, comment. -->
<config version="1.2" enabled="true">
  <server host="127.0.0.1" port="8080">
    <tag>alpha</tag>
    <tag>beta</tag>
  </server>
</config>

bat — sample.yaml

# Palette sample: yaml — comments, keys, strings, numbers, lists, anchors.
name: palette-demo
version: 1.2
enabled: true
defaults: &defaults
  retries: 3
  timeout: "30s"
server:
  <<: *defaults
  host: 0.0.0.0
  ports:
    - 80
    - 443

delta — sample.diff

Δ greeter.go
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

─────┐
• 1: │
─────┘
  1 ⋮  1 │package main

  2 ⋮    │const maxRetries = 3
    ⋮  2 │const maxRetries = 5

  3 ⋮  3 │// Greet returns a greeting for the given name.
  4 ⋮    │func Greet(name string) string {
  5 ⋮    │        return "hi " + name
    ⋮  4 │func Greet(name string) (string, error) {
    ⋮  5 │        if name == "" {
    ⋮  6 │                return "", errEmptyName
    ⋮  7 │        }
    ⋮  8 │        return "hello " + name, nil
  6 ⋮  9 │}

    9     private const int MaxRetries = 3;

    1 """Palette sample: Python — docstrings, strings, numbers, keywords, types."""
    2 from __future__ import annotations
    3 
    4 MAX_RETRIES: int = 3
    5 
    6 
    7 class Greeter:
    8     """Builds greetings."""
    9 
   10     def __init__(self, name: str = "world") -> None:
W  11         self.name = name     ■ Type annotation for attribute `name` is required because this class is not decorated with `@final`
   12 
   13     def greet(self) -> str:
   14         parts = [f"hello {self.name} ({i})\n" for i in range(MAX_RETRIES)]
   15         return "".join(parts)
   16 
   17 
   18 if __name__ == "__main__":
   19     print(Greeter("palette").greet())

    1 #!/usr/bin/env bash
    2 # Palette sample: shell — comments, strings, vars, keywords, numbers.
    3 set -euo pipefail
    4 
    5 readonly MAX_RETRIES=3
    6 greeting="hello\tworld" # escape inside double quotes
    7 
    8 retry() {
    9   local -i attempt=0
   10   while ((attempt < MAX_RETRIES)); do
   11     if "$@"; then
   12       return 0
   13     fi
   14     ((attempt += 1))
   15     printf 'retry %d of %d\n' "$attempt" "$MAX_RETRIES" >&2
   16   done
   17   return 1
   18 }
   19

nvim — sample.tsx

    1 // Palette sample: TSX — types, strings, numbers, keywords, JSX, attributes.
E   2 import { useState } from "react";     ■ Cannot find module 'react' or its corresponding type declarations.
    3 
    4 interface Props {
    5   name?: string;
    6   retries: number;
    7 }
    8 
E   9 export function Greeter({ name = "world", retries }: Props): JSX.Element {     ■ Cannot find namespace 'JSX'.
   10   const [count, setCount] = useState<number>(0);
   11   const label = `hello ${name} (${count}/${retries})`;
   12 
   13   return (
E  14     <button     ■■ JSX element implicitly has type 'any' because no interface 'JSX.IntrinsicElements' exists.
   15       className="card"
   16       disabled={count >= retries}
E  17       onClick={() => setCount((c) => c + 1)}     ■ Parameter 'c' implicitly has an 'any' type.
   18     >
   19       {label}
E  20     </button>     ■ JSX element implicitly has type 'any' because no interface 'JSX.IntrinsicElements' exists.
   21   );
   22 }
